title: Moraea reflexa
taxonomy: Moraea reflexa is classified within the family Iridaceae. The genus Moraea itself is part of the tribe Irideae. This plant is native to South Africa, which is also where it is commonly found. Moraea reflexa is often placed in the subsection Spatae within the genus Moraea. The classification of this species is also recognized in the APG III system.

intro: Moraea reflexa, also known as the reflex moraea or red-breasted moraea, is a flowering plant species in the family Iridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Moraea reflexa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and skin conditions.
